title: Compose Specification, Services, Projects, Dependencies, and Lifecycle · Delivery Case: Docker Compose Service Image Build
summary: A web, worker, and database stack needs one portable declared model with predictable naming, dependencies, health gates, and teardown. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose service image build increment in validated multi-service Compose application model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define image or build source, command, entrypoint, environment, user, working directory, health, resources, and metadata per service.
Resolve project name, working directory, files, includes, merges, interpolation, environment sources, and canonical config output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example three-service project has hidden interpolation, name collisions, start-order assumptions, a mutable image, and teardown that may delete intake data. Render the model, dry-run lifecycle, and prove preservation. Competency probe: Define image or build source, command, entrypoint, environment, user, working directory, health, resources, and metadata per service. A defensible solution applies Docker Compose Service Image Build, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to validated multi-service Compose application model.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-compose-model-lifecycle-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-compose-model-lifecycle-5
    artifact: compose.yaml
    decision: worked docker compose model lifecycle 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      image: registry.example.test/intake@sha256:verified
      depends_on:
      db:
      condition: service_healthy
      volumes:
      intake-data:
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker compose --dry-run up --build
    failure: Reject start order as readiness proof, implicit project identity, floating images, or teardown that silently deletes required volumes.
    repair: Render the merged model, pin identities, encode health dependencies, inspect the dry run, and document down versus down --volumes.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Specifying both build and image always causes Compose to build and ignore registry state. This fails the Docker Compose Service Image Build contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
